### A2-2 Block

- Large site/model plan shows the A2-2 block arranged along a broad curved internal street.
- The composition includes a central linear water feature or blue axis leading from the road edge into a plaza, with paired residential blocks around it.
- The northern/back edge is shown as a dark green landscape or park zone, with paths linking to building clusters.
- Bottom image strip contains four model-detail photographs focused on the plaza, tower bases, curved circulation, and open-space nodes. No separate captions are legible on this page.

## PDF p.124

![PDF page 124](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-124.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

- Upper rendering caption: 근린공원에 연계된 중앙공공보행통로.
- Rendering shows a public pedestrian corridor with water, planting, seating, and mid-rise residential buildings framing the passage.
- Sketch at mid-right shows a pedestrian street or internal open-space view framed by towers.
- Bottom band shows facade/elevation studies of residential buildings, with vertical tower forms and lower connecting elements.

Visible planning statements:

- 도시, 문화, 자연이 하모니를 이루는 경관 및 특성화 계획: 영역별 특성화를 통한 주거유형 설정 and facade composition matching unity and district character.
- 자연과 공생이 이루어지는 생태마을 만들기: green systems connected to 청계산, 금토산, and 중앙공원, with organic connection of outdoor spaces around the internal central plaza.
- 주거환경과의 유기적 관계를 투영하는 Holographic Object: specialized residential buildings planned in harmony with surrounding landscape.

## PDF p.125

![PDF page 125](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-125.jpg)

### A2-2 Block

Four model-view captions:

- 단지 동측.
- 단지 서측.
- 중앙진입 광장.
- 공공보행 연결.

Visible concept text:

- 자연의 향유: brings the surrounding natural environment into the complex through green landscape-oriented residential buildings, piloti that communicate with the natural park at low levels, and Vista Point residential buildings at key site points.
- 너른 마당의 생성: creates a central plaza at the center of the site, with residential blocks enclosing it and looking toward the internal landscape. Support facilities are distributed in connection with the plaza to stabilize the center of the complex.
- 생활가로에 대응: forms the complex entrance in response to the living street and places community facilities in the lower parts of buildings facing the street to activate the street edge.
- Inset rendering caption: 생활가로변 전경.

## PDF p.127

### PAN GYO HOUSING Competition: A8-1 BLOCK

- Architect/team: 종합건축사사무소 ITM.
- Names shown: 이한용 / 고봉철.
- Main concept heading: `'흐름의 회복'`.
- Legible concept summary: the site is described as an "이음매" in the west Pangyo district circulation-street system. The proposal begins from the idea of naturally following the urban street-system flow, forming internal streets from existing street flows, and creating community cores at resulting nodes. The intended effect is to draw the district 생활가로 into the complex and connect the circulation loop.
- Small perspective sketch shows an apartment cluster with curving internal circulation.
- Bottom concept-strip captions: 가로의 형성; 단지 커뮤니티 코어; 생활가로의 연결; 자연요소의 유입; 단지의 완결성 부여.

# A8-1 Block Completion, B3-1 Block, And B6-1 Block Opening

Coverage note: PDF pp.131-132 complete the A8-1 Block public-sale material with model views, community facility plans, and an amenity/elevation strip. PDF pp.133-138 cover the B3-1 Block housing competition entry and its project statistics, site/rendering sheets, layout principles, elevations, and community facility drawings. PDF pp.139-140 begin the B6-1 Block entry with its design concept and project statistics.

## PDF p.131

![PDF page 131](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-131.jpg)

### A8-1 Block

The page presents model photographs and a short design narrative for the A8-1 Block. Readable image captions:

- `단지 동측`: model view from the east.
- `단지 서측`: model view from the west.
- `단지 전경`: overall model view.
- `단지 진입부 전경`: entry-area model view.
- `생활가로변 야경`: night rendering along the living street.

Readable narrative themes:

- `자연의 유입`: introduces a green axis linked to a natural park on the northeast side of the site, with level planning and skyline control intended to respond to the formed landform and surrounding mountain scenery.
- `도시경관에 대응`: plans symbolic residential buildings responding to the urban street, differentiating building forms by urban, street-facing, and foothill zones to create a distinctive and dynamic landscape.
- `인간친화`: emphasizes a landscape-centered complex, nearby residential buildings and exterior spaces, and areas facing the living street and foothill edge; unit living spaces are differentiated by function and treated as higher-quality spaces.

Figure notes: the four large photos document massing, tower placement, internal open space, and entry sequence; the small night rendering records the intended street-edge lighting and skyline effect.

## PDF p.132

![PDF page 132](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-132.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

This page shows facility floor plans and a long site/elevation strip. A small `KEY MAP` appears at upper left.

Readable plan headings:

- `관리사무소`: management office floor plan.
- `근린생활시설`: neighborhood living facility plan.
- `휘트니스 & 스파`: fitness and spa plan.
- `경로당`: senior center plan.

Bottom strip labels and amenity notes:

- `수변휴게공간`: includes `맑은쉼터` and `꽃내음놀이터`.
- `인포넷센터`: shown below a residential tower segment.
- `나눔마당`: includes `건강마당` and `물내음놀이터`.
- `주민복지시설`: another tower-base community facility marker.
- `청연의언덕`: includes `푸른쉼터`.

Figure notes: the floor plans contain many room names and dimensions, but the microtext is visually dense at this page resolution. The bottom strip is useful for understanding the sequence of waterside rest space, information/community facilities, shared yards, welfare facilities, and hill/open-space amenities.

## PDF p.133

![PDF page 133](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-133.jpg)

### PAN GYO HOUSING Competition: B3-1 BLOCK

Design office and team as printed:

- `(주)신한 종합건축사사무소`
- `조재우 / 조명규 / 김혁기 / 장진영`
- `김진옥 / 김정우 / 장홍석 / 백성암`

Readable design statement: `B3-1블럭은 자연녹지에 둘러싸여 단독주택지를 바라보는 형상` and is explained through three large ideas: linkage among elements, community grouping, and topographic adaptation. The text describes the complex as mediating between nature and culture through open edges, a centralizing interior organization, and internal streets. Four community groups around the central plaza create different spatial characters and give territorial identity to the whole complex. The overall site layout follows existing terrain and surrounding conditions, distinguishing hillside/flatland and city/nature conditions while avoiding a closed complex form.

Concept diagram captions:

- `GREEN AXIS`
- `WATER FLOW`
- `OPEN SPACE`
- `COMMUNITY GROUP`
- `VEHICLE ROUTE`

Figure notes: the five small diagrams summarize landscape, water, open-space, community, and circulation systems for B3-1 Block. Fine internal map labels are visually dense.

## PDF p.135

### B3-1 Block

The main image is a dark master plan/rendered site plan with a north arrow. It shows low-rise residential buildings organized around a curved central pedestrian/landscape spine, wooded edges, and roads along the perimeter.

Readable figure captions along the bottom:

- `옥외엘리베이터 & 부대시설`: model detail of the outdoor elevator and support facilities.
- `클러스터주동`: model detail of clustered residential buildings.
- `테라스주동`: model detail of terrace-type buildings.
- `자연개방형 주동`: model detail of nature-open residential buildings.

Figure notes: this page is primarily visual. The large plan clarifies the relationship between the sloped/wooded setting, a central plaza or pedestrian route, and differentiated residential building types.

## PDF p.136

![PDF page 136](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-136.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

Readable image captions:

- `중앙광장변 테라스주동 전경`: view of terrace buildings along the central plaza.
- `중앙광장 전경`: view of the central plaza.
- `판상형 입면`: elevation drawing for the slab-type housing.

Readable layout text: `저층 연립주택인 B3-1블럭은 기존지형과 주변여건에 따라 4가지의 배치형상을 가지고 있다.`

Four layout forms:

- `테라스주택의 중첩 배치`: a layout responding to the northern hillside, composed of terrace housing facing the street and central plaza plus row housing at the summit.
- `판상형주택의 개방 배치`: radial arrangement drawing southern natural elements into the center of the complex and considering views from the buildings.
- `가로를 향해 열린 클러스터 배치`: buildings respond actively to the urban street, emphasizing linkage rather than separation.
- `단지완결성을 부여하는 클러스터 배치`: community groups at the edge of the site give completion to the whole complex.

Figure notes: the photos and elevation support the low-rise terrace/row-house identity and the central plaza as a shared open-space anchor.

- `중앙광장 전경`: water space and outdoor elevators form a nature-friendly plaza.
- `중앙광장 야경`: lighting planning creates a bright and cozy nightscape.

## PDF p.138

![PDF page 138](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-138.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

This page focuses on B3-1 Block community and neighborhood facilities. A small `KEY MAP` locates three facility groups.

Readable facility descriptions:

- `주민공동시설-1`: integrated with a residential building and placed at the main complex entrance to secure accessibility. Listed uses are `인포넷 센터` and `카페테리아`.
- `주민공동시설-2`: located below terrace buildings, wrapping the main pedestrian road inside the complex and forming a central space for users. Listed uses are `경로당`, `관리사무소`, and `피트니스 센터`.
- `주민공동시설-3 근린생활시설`: located below buildings facing the urban street, forming the pedestrian streetscape and maximizing outside access and use. Listed components are `주민공동시설 1`, `주민공동시설 2`, and `근린생활시설`.

Bottom caption:

- `하부 부대시설 및 상가 입면`: elevation of lower support facilities and commercial frontage.

Figure notes: the right side contains detailed floor plans with dimensions and room labels. The major facility headings are legible; room-level microtext and dimension strings are visually dense and should be checked from a higher-resolution source if needed.

## PDF p.139

![PDF page 139](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-139.jpg)

### PAN GYO HOUSING Competition: B6-1 BLOCK

Design office and team as printed:

- `(주)종합건축사사무소 건원`
- `김종국 / 함인선 / 송현석 / 유창협`

Readable design statement: the proposal begins from the purchaser's intent for housing. It frames buyers of roughly 50-pyeong terrace houses in a quiet forested hillside at the edge of Pangyo New Town as seeking something between isolated detached/country houses and apartment-complex ideas of dense community and safety. The text warns that, depending on programming, construction, and resident use, the result could become a gathering of the strengths of detached houses and apartments or merely a list of their weaknesses.

Topographic organization:

- The site is divided into three areas by slope: a steep hillside area, a gentle slope area, and a nearly flat step area.
- The steep area uses independent terrace houses as point types, forming a `리조트 빌리지`.
- The gentle slope area uses row-type terrace houses with shared cores for two households, forming a `힐사이드 빌리지`.
- The nearly flat stepped area uses area-type `이너코트(Inner-court) 빌리지`.
- Each cluster is organized as 2-4 clusters to make an appropriate neighborhood grouping, with community decks as the center of neighbor formation.
- Three villages are horizontally connected by a community axis at the same level, while vertical side paths plug into community units. The text describes identity through point, line, and plane housing types.

Concept diagram captions and labels:

- `Nature & Urban`
- `녹지 및 수체계의 보존`
- `지형의 보존`
- Visible English labels include `NATURE`, `URBAN`, `ECO RING`, `SLOPE`, `EDGE`, `STEP`, `SLOPETYPE`, `EDGE TYPE`, and `STEP TYPE`.

Figure notes: the diagrams emphasize the relationship of nature and urban fabric, preservation of green/water systems, and preservation/adaptation of slope types.

# B6-1 Block / 단위세대 특화방안

Coverage note: PDF pages 141-146 continue the B6-1 Block design package for 성남판교 휴먼시아, emphasizing hillside housing, community spine, ecological water/green networks, and resident welfare facilities. PDF pages 147-150 shift to August-sale unit and finish-specialization measures, including exterior total design, site-entry facilities, minus-option finishes, and furniture design competition procedures.

## PDF p.141

![PDF page 141](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-141.jpg)

### B6-1 Block

- Full-page presentation board with a large illuminated site/model plan for B6-1 Block.
- The main image shows clustered residential buildings organized along curving circulation, stepped green decks, small water features, and a central round plaza-like node.
- Left-side model-photo captions visible:
  - "우드데크"
  - "단지입구 에코타워 전경"
  - "중앙광장"
  - "내부통경축"
- Fine labels embedded in the large plan are visually dense and not reliably readable at page-image resolution.

## PDF p.142

![PDF page 142](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-142.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

- Upper-right running title: "성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)".
- Main rendering caption: "HillSide Village".
- Small concept diagram title appears as "자연과 인공물과의 공생".
- Concept diagram organizes Nature / Building / Symbiosis across terrain, green, and blue-network themes. Readable labels include English/Korean pairings such as "Slope", "Forest", "Stream", "Coverage", "Route", "Interweave", and "Coincidence"; smaller Korean labels are dense.

Visible design prose:

> 경사 지역의 주택이 갖는 다양한 지형의 연출을 이용하여 마당의 깊이를 강조할 수 있다. 각 주동의 사이에 둔 인터라움(Inter-raum)은 숲과 마당이 만나는 장으로서 전통 한옥에서 마당과 후원 숲 사이의 대청마루가 소통하듯 '바람이 지나는 집'의 개념을 가진다. 이 공간은 주택 내부 또는 주택 사이사이의 마당들이 외부 공간, 곧 수목이나 숲들과 교감하는 만남의 장이며, 이 개념은 데크의 패치(Patch) 개념을 극대화시킨다. 클러스터 사이의 데크는 다양한 프로그램이 조각보처럼 직조되는데 치마선이 서로 달라 마당이 깊은 집이 되었다. 외부 공간 계획은 트리하우징(Tree Housing)의 디자인 개념이 적용되었다. 즉 나무그늘은 캐노피로서 인간을 품는 쉼터를 제공하는 역할을 하고, 잎들은 자연환경을 조화롭게 흡수할 수 있는 매개체로서, 가지들은 자연 보행 예술 문화 네트워킹으로서 뿌리는 네트워킹의 집합과 확산이라는 결정점이 되었다.

- Bottom image band caption: "경관 입면"; it shows a long elevation/landscape section with residential blocks against a wooded slope and an architectural feature in the center.

## PDF p.144

![PDF page 144](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-144.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아 (8월 공공분양)

- Upper renderings:
  - "Inner Court View"
  - "Community Spine View"
- Small concept diagram title appears to describe the symbiosis between 공동체 의식 and 사적 생활. The diagram compares Community / Private Living / Symbiosis across open space, pedestrian, and neighborhood patterns; readable English labels include "Community", "Loggia", "Liquid", "Mesh", "Hierarchical Tree", "Semi-Lattice", "Convergence", "Divergence", and "Community Scaling".

Visible design prose:

> 단지를 조성하는 건축 개념은 자연과 인공물의 공생에 두었다. 따라서 경사지를 절토하여 넓은 평면을 만든 다음 건물을 앉히는 방식은 지양되었다. 대신 지형을 따라 접힘(Folding) 또는 기대기(Leaning) 같은 개념으로 주택을 경사지에 얹는 시도를 하였고, 숲과 녹지를 이른바 '까서' 그 밀어낸 자리만큼을 건축물로 건폐(Coverage)하는 개념 대신 숲과 숲 사이에 주택을 짜 넣는 짜깁기(Interweave)라는 개념으로 프로그램이 진행되었다.

- Bottom section/elevation bands show sectional relationships among "생태연못", "Community CAFE", "Inner Court Villa", "Sunken Garden", "Nature", "10m 도로", "Buffer Green", "Loggia Villa", "Multi-Alleys", and "Community Center".

|---|---|---|---|
| 주출입구 외관 | 석재, 금속 등을 이용한 일반적인 동출입구 고급화 | 주민의 Identity 고착; 계획시점의 디자인 경쟁 도입이 잦아지고 있고 석재 등을 이용한 특화계획은 이미 특화가 아닌 일반화가 되고 있음. More: 단지전체의 디자인 Concept과의 일치, 옥탑/동 Mass 등 통일된 디자인 필요, 차별화 | Entry exterior render/photo references |
| 주출입구 내관 | 내부의 고급화 시도(타사례) | 주출입구 내부의 Lobby 공간화. More: 동출입구의 구조적 문제를 탈피하고 Lobby의 개념을 어느 정도, 누가 먼저 도입하느냐가 관건 | Interior lobby/detail reference images |

## PDF p.149

![PDF page 149](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-149.jpg)

### 마감재 마이너스 옵션제 시범적용

Visible explanatory text:

- 견본주택에 제시된 마감 외에, 입주 후 마감재를 철거하지 않고 소비자 취향에 맞는 인테리어를 입주자가 개별적으로 시공할 수 있도록 일명 "누드주택"을 선택 옵션으로 제공.
- 발코니 확장형 주택을 기준으로 공사관리, 하자예방, 외관통일을 위한 골조와 기본 바탕재는 주택공사에서 시공하고, 색상 및 디자인 선호가 달라지는 마감재는 입주자가 시공.
- 세대수가 적고 평면형태가 다양한 중대형 연립주택에 시범적용.

#### 적용대상

- 적용블럭: B3-1블럭(170세대), B6-1블럭(206세대)
- 적용평형: 125㎡ ~ 208㎡, 15개 평형

#### 공사범위

| 구분 | 공사범위 |
|---|---|
| 건축 | 벽: 석고보드 및 초배 도배 |
| 건축 | 천정: 석고보드 및 조인트 도배 초배 |
| 건축 | 바닥: 방바닥 미장(마루귀틀 등 매립물 포함), 발코니, 욕실 방수 및 바탕 마감 |
| 건축 | 창호류: 방문틀(기틀), PL창호, 현관방화문, 외부샤시 |
| 건축 | 기타: 우수선 홈통, 각종 드레인 |
| 기계 | 배관, 설비 및 소방 설비 등 법적설치 자재 |
| 전기통신 | 전기 등기구를 제외한 모든 배선 공사 |

#### 공사비

- 공사에서 시공해 주는 발코니 확장형 주택을 기준으로 위 공사범위에 해당하는 품목을 제외한 마감 공사비를 일괄 마이너스 옵션으로 운용.

#### 예시도

- Lower figure is a unit floor-plan example. Visible caption includes "단위세대 평면도 145㎡ B"; small construction-note callouts include a "150T" panel note but the full title is too small to confirm reliably.

### 성남판교 휴먼시아

### 가구류 디자인 공모 실시

Visible explanatory text:

- 인테리어 주요 마감자재인 가구류의 디자인 다양화, 고급화 유도 및 수납공간 최적화를 위해 가구류 디자인 경쟁을 실시하고 지급자재로 운용.
- 견본주택 적용가구와 동일제품이 현장 시공됨으로써 품질향상 유도.

Visible bullets:

- 적용지구: A19-1등 5개BL 2,066호(25개 평면타입)
- 대상자재: 주방가구, 신발장, 반침가구 등 가구류
- 디자인 공모 절차

#### 디자인 공모 절차

```text
평형별 토탈 인테리어 디자인 제시
↓
가구류 디자인 공모
(13개업체참여, 4개업체 디자인(안)선정)
↓
평형별 디자인 확정
(기준도서 작성)
↓
가격경쟁 입찰
(4개업체 중 2개업체 선정)
↓
지급자재 운용
(5개블럭 2,066호를 2개군으로 분류)
```

- Right-side reference images show furniture/interior presentation sheets and mockup imagery. Readable English phrases include "FURNITURE" and "154 PENT Black Charisma"; smaller presentation-board labels are dense.

# 성남판교 휴먼시아 - 주민커뮤니티 시설계획 및 단위세대계획/인테리어

Coverage note: visual review of PDF pp.151-160 only. This batch covers the resident community facility concept, West/East Pangyo community amenity planning, apartment unit plans for 84/101/118/162 types, and four interior concept boards.

## PDF p.151

![PDF page 151](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-151.jpg)

### 주민커뮤니티 시설계획

Visible headings:

- `커뮤니티 시설의 설계 개념`
- `연계시설물의 조닝 및 그룹핑`
- `입지분석에 따른 지역별 커뮤니티 컨셉 적용`

The main concept panel groups `COMMUNITY`, `AMENITY`, and `IDENTITY` into five amenity families:

| Group | Visible program labels | Visible purpose text |
|---|---|---|
| WELLBEING | `FITNESS, GOLF, SPA` | `육체적 건강과 정신적 건강의 조화를 위한 웰빙시설` |
| EDUCATION | `INFO-NET 센터` | `정보검색, 도서열람, 독서 생활정보 나눔공간` |
| CULTURE | `주민카페, 멀티프로그램실(취미,교양)` | `동호회, 교육, 취미활동 등 다양한 주민자치활동 수용` |
| SILVER | `노인정` | `사회적 소외계층인 노인 복지기능 수용` |
| KIDS | `보육시설` | `탁아기능 및 미취학 유아의 사회적 적응 기능 수용` |

Community positioning diagram:

- Central note: `커뮤니티 활성화`
- Central bullets: `대중적명품이미지(Masstige)부각`; `공영개발 주택단지의 이미지 제고`
- `서판교`: `독립형 블럭`; `단지내 주민위주 커뮤니티시설 계획`; examples shown include `인포넷`, `북티크`, `주민카페`, `휘트니스`, `스파`.
- `동판교`: `생활가로변 블럭`; `외부가로와 연계된 커뮤니티시설 광역화`; `문화, 체육시설 강화`; examples shown include `공원`, `주민문화`, `골프연습장`.
- Supporting visuals include a physical model, community center photos, and street-facing retail/community precedent imagery.

## PDF p.152

![PDF page 152](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-152.jpg)

### 성남판교 휴먼시아

#### 서판교

`아파트 주민위주의 커뮤니티시설 계획`

- `자연환경과 인접한 독립형 블록으로 단지내 주민위주의 커뮤니티시설 계획`
- `피트니스, 주민문화정보공간(Infonet 센터), 다목적실(멀티프로그램실 설치), 주민카페 등 커뮤니티공간계획`

`연립주택 부대복리시설 계획`

- `지형에 순응하는 테라스하우스 등 연립주택 블록으로 단지내 주민위주의 부대복리시설 계획`
- `피트니스, 주민카페 등 입주민의 편의와 커뮤니티를 적극적으로 유도할 수 있는 공간 계획`

#### 동판교

`생활가로와 연계하는 고급스런 부대복리시설 계획`

- `동판교의 남북을 관통하는 생활가로변에 분당 정자동 주상복합 블록내의 상업가로와 유사한 고급스런 상업시설(리테일)과 주민커뮤니티시설을 배치`
- `피트니스, 골프연습장, 주민문화정보공간(Infonet 센터), 다목적실(멀티프로그램실 설치) 등 다양한 커뮤니티 공간이 도시가로와 함께 어우러지도록 계획`
- `노유자를 위한 승강기, 각 건물간 연결브리지 계획으로 부대복리시설간 네트워킹을 구성하여 주민이용의 시너지효과 제고`

Figures: right side shows elevation/section-style facade drawings and perspective renderings for community/amenity buildings. Bottom images show physical site/building models, including a street-edge/community facility model.

## PDF p.156

![PDF page 156](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-156.jpg)

### 휴식 (Fresh & Natural)

## Key Figures And Tables

- PDF p.151: resident community facility concept diagram with five grouped program families: WELLBEING, EDUCATION, CULTURE, SILVER, and KIDS.
- PDF p.152: West/East Pangyo community facility planning page, with facade/elevation drawings, perspective renderings, and physical models.
- PDF p.153: `84A Type` unit plan with additional `84B`, `84C-1`, and `84D-1` plans.
- PDF p.154: `순수 (Pure & Simple)` interior concept board.
- PDF p.155: `101A Type` unit plan with additional `101B1`, `101C`, `101D`, `101E`, and `101F1` plans.
- PDF p.156: `휴식 (Fresh & Natural)` interior concept board.
- PDF p.157: `118B Type` unit plan with additional `118A Type` plan.
- PDF p.158: `명상 (Oriental & Mysterious)` interior concept board.
- PDF p.159: `162 Type` duplex upper/lower floor plans.
- PDF p.160: `격조 (Neutral & Contemporary)` interior concept board.
- No formal tabular data table is visible in this page range; the only table in this batch note is a transcription aid for the p.151 program grouping diagram.

## Stats Candidates

- Community program groups on p.151: `WELLBEING`, `EDUCATION`, `CULTURE`, `SILVER`, `KIDS`.
- Community facility program examples on pp.151-152: fitness, golf, spa, Info-Net/Infonet center, 주민카페, 멀티프로그램실, 노인정, 보육시설, 골프연습장, 주민문화정보공간.
- Unit type labels: `84A`, `84B`, `84C-1`, `84D-1`, `101A`, `101B1`, `101C`, `101D`, `101E`, `101F1`, `118B`, `118A`, and `162 Type`.
- Explicit apartment planning values: `3LDK` for 101A, `5.95m` maximum living-room width for 101A, `전면 5BAY` for 118B, and `복층` for 162 Type.
- The 162 Type plan separates `상부층` and `하부층`, with visible terraces labeled `view terrace`, `family terrace`, and `테라스`.

## Validation Notes

- Reviewed visually from `pdf-page-151.jpg` through `pdf-page-160.jpg`; OCR and image-to-text automation were not used.
- All ten PDF pages in the assigned range have source markers and image links.
- The visible running project label `성남판교 휴먼시아` appears on several right-hand pages; the running footer identifies the chapter as `03 판교신도시 건축계획안`.
- Floor-plan room labels and headline planning bullets were transcribed where legible. Dense millimeter dimension strings and very small drawing annotations were summarized rather than exhaustively normalized.
- Interior concept text on pp.154, 156, 158, and 160 was legible enough to transcribe; photographic content was summarized as figure notes.

## PDF p.167

![PDF page 167](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-167.jpg)

Project heading: `PAN GYO HOUSING Competition`.

Main title: `A17-2,A18-2 BLOCK`.

Design office and credited names:

- `(주)종합건축사사무소 건원`
- `김종국 / 고성호 / 송현석 / 정호윤 / 양남목 / 성상우 / 석정준`

Readable narrative text:

`국민임대단지의 계획주안점은 항상 공사비절감에 있다. 장기간에 걸친 설계기간에도 불구하고 몇차례의 택지 개발계획 변경에 따른 계획안의 수정시 마다 굴곡된 주동은 "-"자로 펴졌으며, 스카이라인의 변화는 주동수를 최소화하기 위해 무시되었다. 단지, 지구단위계획지침에 따른 계획만이 허용되었다.`

`A17-2블럭은 그러한 틀속에서 학교변으로의 개방감과 운중천변으로의 통경축을 확보하기 위하여 대지 우측변으로 타워형주동을 남북으로 배치하였고, 그 주변으로 부대복리시설을 계획하였다. 경로당과 보육시설 및 경비실은 주동 타입의 디자인보다 몇배의 많은 시간이 소요되며 섬세하게 디자인 되었는데, 작은규모임에도 불구하고 단지의 중심적인 랜드마크가 되리라고 기대한다.`

`A18-2블럭은 상부의 18-1블럭과 연계되어 대지의 한가운데에 초고층타워형이 배치되었고, 좌측의 생활가로변은 저층과 고층의 판상형조합으로 Human Scale에 대응하도록 하였다. 대지 우측으로는 고속화도로변의 지루한 직각 배치에 변화를 주고자 Void된 공지를 계획함으로써 Urban Scale에 대한 배려를 하였다. 생활가로변의 부대시설은 매스를 분절하고, 그 분절된 매스를 Corridor로 연결함으로서 가로변의 표정에 활력을 주고자 노력하였다.`

Bottom sketch captions:

- `생활가로`
- `운중천변 전경`
- `중심가로변`
- `외부공원 연계`

# PDF Pages 171-180: A18-2BL, A24-1 Block, A25-1 Block

Coverage note: this batch visually reviews the closing presentation sheets for A18-2BL, then the A24-1 Block and A25-1 Block housing competition entries. The pages contain site plans, model photographs, facility drawings, Korean captions, and two readable project-statistics blocks for 성남판교 휴먼시아 국민임대 housing.

## PDF p.171

Content notes:

- Left column shows three colored plan/model images labeled `현상안`, `최초안`, and a final adjustment image whose label appears to be `최종조정안`.
- Main drawing is labeled `배치도`.
- The site plan emphasizes north-south internal circulation, several residential clusters, and open-space nodes.
- Readable plan labels include `보행자 주출입구`, repeated `주출입구`, `어린이공원`, `어린이놀이터`, `주민운동시설`, `근린생활시설`, `상가주차`, `지하주차`, `대지경계`, and `건축한계선`.
- Multiple parking-count labels are visible, including `주차:32대`, `주차:34대`, `주차:43대`, `주차:44대`, `주차:46대`, and `지하주차:332대`.
- Building labels include repeated unit/floor notations such as `46-75-14-10F`, `46-75-11F`, and similar apartment-unit strings; the full microtext is visually dense.

## PDF p.177

![PDF page 177](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-177.jpg)

Visible heading: `PAN GYO HOUSING Competition` / `A25-1 BLOCK`.

Project credit:

- `(주)희림 종합건축사사무소`
- `정영균 / 백승진 / 김경순 / 정만기 / 최지영 / 이영수 / 양승희`

Visible concept phrase: `"풍경 속의 풍경"`.

Readable narrative points:

- The concept aims to create a residential complex harmonized with the surrounding landscape.
- The text describes a green residential district in Pangyo that uses nature as a backdrop and also considers panoramic views from Bundang.
- It states that the 국민임대 site lies in a long narrow parcel parallel to `분당수서간 고속도로`.
- Three concept elements are named: `친환경`, `친인간`, and `친경관`.
- Environmental planning includes village woods connected to buffer greenery, distinctive tree species, three residential clusters, east-west open spaces, wind paths, and view corridors.
- Human-centered planning includes row-type housing along the west `25m 생활가로변` and linear ground-floor welfare/support facilities to create inter-block community exchange.
- Landscape planning includes high-rise tower groups at the north entry for identity and coordination of mid-rise and high-rise apartment masses along the expressway edge.

Bottom caption strip:

- `마을숲구상도-1`
- `마을숲구상도-2`

# 국제설계경기 프로젝트 도입 및 B5-1BL

Coverage note: PDF pages 181-190 transition from the preceding architectural-planning project pages into `국제설계경기 프로젝트`. The range includes the international competition contents page, background and schedule pages, the winning-entry jury report, and the opening B5-1 Block winner material by Helin & Co Architects with (주)아이아크 건축사사무소. Review was visual only; dense plan microtext and very small team-name text are summarized or marked as visually dense.

## PDF p.183

![PDF page 183](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-183.jpg)

### 국제현상 도입배경

#### 사업지 대상블록 현황

Visible background text says the project followed the government's real-estate policy dated `05.8.31` and sought to invite international architects into the publicly developed Seongnam-Pangyo district, aiming for new housing complexes with architectural aesthetics/philosophy and advancement of housing culture.

#### 위치

- `경기도 성남시 분당구 판교동 일원`
- `판교택지개발지구 내 공동주택 B5-1, B5-2, B5-3 3개 블럭`

#### 건설규모

| 블록 | 대지면적 (㎡) | 건설호수 (호) | 평균 평형 규모 (전용+공용면적 평) | 허용용적률 | 최고층수 (이하) | 유형 |
|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---|---|
| B5-1 | 28,594 | 98 | 연립주택: 50평; 테라스하우스: 75평 | 65% | 4층 | 분양 |
| B5-2 | 29,135 | 100 | Same as above, indicated by ditto mark | 65% | Same as above, indicated by ditto mark | 분양 |
| B5-3 | 29,798 | 102 | Same as above, indicated by ditto mark | 65% | Same as above, indicated by ditto mark | 분양 |

#### 주변현황

- Located about `20km` from central Seoul and about `10km` from Gangnam.
- The text describes the site as a southern Seoul-metropolitan transport node where the Gyeongbu Expressway and Seoul Outer Ring Expressway meet, with strong regional accessibility.
- The district is adjacent to Bundang New Town and within about `10km` of Gwangju, Gwacheon, Uiwang, Suwon, and Yongin.
- The project area is described as lying mainly in the western Pangyo district between the Seoul Outer Ring Expressway and Local Road 57, using the Gyeongbu Expressway as reference.

#### 입지여건

| Direction | Visible Adjacent Conditions |
|---|---|
| 동 | 양재-영덕간 도로(고가), 체육공원, 공동주택지, 금토산 |
| 서 | 한국정신문화원 사택(2층), 취락지 |
| 남 | 도시계획도로(20m), 단독주택지, 국지도57호선, 운중천 |
| 북 | 근린공원, 서울외곽순환도로, 청계산 |

#### 지형지세

- Average elevation is about `110m`.
- The project district includes natural hilly land reaching about `130m`.
- South-side planned road formation height is shown as `94~100m`.
- Existing land use: `전, 대지, 임야`.
- Slope: `10°~25°` south-facing slope.

#### 지반여건

- Ground is described mainly as silty sand, weathered soil, and weathered rock.
- Bedrock layer `(연암)` is expected below about elevation `100m`.
- Along the north neighborhood-park edge of B5-1, soft-rock strata are expected around `105~120m` below/level context; some rock occurrence is expected during cutting.

Figure note:

- Right-side locator map highlights `사업지 대상블록` and labels `B5-1`, `B5-2`, and `B5-3`.

## PDF p.185

![PDF page 185](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-185.jpg)

### 당선작 심사 보고서

#### 심사평

- The jury statement identifies the competition goal as developing a new, innovative, publicity-worthy model for low-rise collective housing.
- The visible review names `페카 헬린(Pekka Helin)`, `야마모토 리켄(Yamamoto Riken)`, and `마크 맥(Mark Mack)` as winning entries for the B5-1, B5-2, and B5-3 blocks arranged east-west.
- The review describes the competition as well organized and the brief/site as timely, ambitious, and capable of supporting design diversity and potential.
- It notes that the submissions showed careful analysis, refined presentation methods, and attention to environmental issues.
- Remaining tasks include improving unit plans in relation to Korean traditions/lifestyles, reconsidering accessibility for physically weaker users even where terrace houses are legally treated as first-floor dwellings, and securing identity/recognizability for individual units in the Mark Mack and Pekka Helin schemes.

#### 페카 헬린 - B5-1 Block Winner

- The text says this entry creates a distinctive `장소` and uses flowing north-European curved lines to arrange housing blocks along the site's dramatic natural character.
- Jury concerns include insufficient open-space/community definition at appropriate scale and worry that the `Ribbons of Housing` may read as a very large green-roofed building.
- Ecological strengths include planting plans on roofs and terraces, but the text notes that maintaining these planted elements would require separate community rules.
- Most housing blocks face south, producing favorable view and daylight conditions, while individual unit plans require further improvement.
- The flowing arrangement is described as harmonious with existing natural surroundings, though it may require more cut-and-fill than other schemes and therefore raises ecological concerns.
- The text also calls for careful separation of vehicular and pedestrian circulation.

Figure notes:

- Lower-right competition board includes plans, perspectives, unit diagrams, and the visible English board text `PANGYO HOUSING COMPETITION | FLOW`.
- Caption: `▲ 페카 헬린(Pekka Helin) Helin & Co Architects, 핀란드 + (주)아이아크 건축사사무소`.
- Lower-left faint map image marks the B5-1 project position within the district.

## PDF p.186

![PDF page 186](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-186.jpg)

### 야마모토 리켄 - B5-2 Block Winner

- The jury text says the Yamamoto Riken proposal arranges units as clusters around public space, creating clearer common spaces.
- Building mass and neighborhood-cluster scale are described as well integrated with the site.
- The open spaces between housing blocks are linked to Korea National Housing Corporation's visible motto of creating a `인간적인 공동체`.
- The plan uses residential blocks around a shared deck; visible terms include `시키(Shikii)` and `마루`.
- The deck level supports flexibility, with sunken courtyards below, but the review notes concerns about outward views and market reaction to sunken gardens for ventilation/daylight below deck level.
- Caption: `▲ 야마모토 리켄(Riken Yamamoto) Riken Yamamoto & Field Shop, 일본 + (주)종합건축사사무소 건원`.

### 마크 맥 - B5-3 Block Winner

- The Mark Mack proposal is praised for minimizing damage to the existing natural setting and creating varied exterior public spaces and private gardens.
- Interior views and exterior landscape are described as carefully considered.
- The jury values the arrangement of housing blocks around private gardens/courtyards with south-facing orientation.
- Courtyard-type units are identified as the strongest of the three housing types in the scheme.
- The `ㄴ`-shaped blocks secure views and south-facing placement while making strong use of the site's advantages.
- The review raises concern about the linear residential arrangement on the northern side: it may block noise from the north but also risks cutting off green space behind the buildings.
- Caption: `▲ 마크 맥(Mark Mack) MACK firm, 미국 + (주)종합건축사사무소 동우건축`.

Figure notes:

- Left competition board shows colored cluster plans, sections, diagrams, and rendered housing/courtyard images.
- Right competition board shows a large rendered perspective of low-rise terrace housing, a site/master plan, unit plan diagrams, and sections.

## PDF p.187

![PDF page 187](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-187.jpg)

### PANGYO HOUSING Competition - B5-1 BLOCK

#### Design Team

Visible international team:

- `Helin & Co Architects`
- `Pekka Helin / Satu Jaatinen / Antti Korkkula / Jonni Laitto / Virve Vuolasvirta / Riitta Soininen`
- `Tommi Sassi / Jari Huhtaniemi / Landscape Design / Pia Kuusiniemi / Krista Muurinen`

Visible Korean team:

- `(주)아이아크 건축사사무소`
- Readable names include `하태석`, `조광일`, `정종헌`, `김정삼`, `김석천`, `정혁`, `정구현`, and `양은주`; one short Korean personal name in the middle is visually small/uncertain.

#### Diagrams And Captions

- `투시도`: bird's-eye view of green-roofed, curving terrace housing in a wooded slope.
- `Green Zone`: plan diagram highlighting green/forest and circulation zones.
- `Flow : 단지`: green curving bars showing the housing-block flow.
- `Flow : 오픈 스페이스`: orange network diagram showing open-space flow.
- `Flow : 바람`: fine line diagram showing wind-flow paths.
- `캐노피`: plan diagram marking canopy points in red.

#### 판교 하우징 FLOW

- The bottom green text describes `판교 하우징 FLOW` as a joint design by Helsinki-based Helin & Co and Seoul-based iArc Architects.
- Helin & Co handled housing-block and unit-plan design; iArc developed the overall housing-site concept and communication/design coordination.
- The collaboration is described as iterative, using email and telephone communication before a design meeting in Tokyo.
- The site concept developed from iArc's system diagrams into an overall district plan that accommodated Helin & Co's architectural plan.
- The design strategy is to maximize the value of the existing sloped site, treating the slope as an advantage rather than a defect.
- The text criticizes common large-scale earthwork that flattens Korean slopes and disconnects residential space from nature.

### 국제설계경기 프로젝트 - B5-1BL

#### Land Use Key Map

- Small map labeled `LAND USE KEY MAP` highlights the B5-1 location in green.
- Large rendering shows curved, terraced low-rise housing with planted roofs and terraces embedded in a wooded night landscape.

#### Project Statistics

| Item | Visible Value |
|---|---:|
| 세대수 | 총 세대수 98세대 |
| 대지면적 | 28,594.00㎡ |
| 건축면적 | 9,271.06㎡ |
| 연면적 | 28,372.71㎡ |
| 건폐율 | 32.42% |
| 용적률 | 64.89% (18,555.98㎡) |
| 도로율 | 10.90% (3,120.00㎡) |
| 녹지면적률 | 31.70% (9,064.29㎡) |
| 자연지반녹지율 | 19.50% (5,575.80㎡) |

#### Concept Text Notes

- Housing blocks flow along the site slope, and gaps between blocks naturally form public spaces.
- B5-1 is described as having two public spaces with different characters: a forested hilltop community center and a lower play-yard/park.
- The hilltop community center provides an important visual identity for the complex, appearing as a wooded hill.
- Two building types are described: `테라스하우스` and `멀티패밀리하우스`.
- Terrace houses adapt to the slope in section and create private terraces.
- Multi-family houses follow a related principle but have smaller terraces.
- The text states that although the scheme starts from three basic unit types, variations produce different units; the shared trait is outward orientation with views.
- Terraces include planted edges, supporting greening at unit level and protecting privacy below.
- Author credit visible at the end: `글 : 하태석 Tesoc Hah`.

## Validation Notes

- Visual source pages reviewed: `pdf-page-181.jpg` through `pdf-page-190.jpg`.
- OCR was not used. No image-to-text automation was used.
- All ten pages have explicit `## PDF p.XXX` source markers and direct image links.
- Numeric tables and statistics were transcribed from visible page panels on pages 183, 184, 188, and 190.
- Dense plan microtext, contour labels, and some very small personal/team names were not guessed; the page image links preserve the source for later high-resolution checking.
- Section transition captured: page 181 is a mostly blank visual transition, and page 182 opens `국제설계경기 프로젝트`.

# 국제설계경기 프로젝트: B5-1 Block / B5-2 Block

Coverage note: PDF pages 191-198 continue the B5 hillside residential scheme with public-space, community-facility, terrace-house, multifamily-house, landscape, circulation, and approval-plan material. PDF pages 199-200 begin the `PANGYO HOUSING Competition B5-2 BLOCK` proposal by Riken Yamamoto & Field Shop and Kunwon.

## PDF p.191

![PDF page 191](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-191.jpg)

- Visible headings/captions: `퍼블릭 스페이스 Public Space`, `퍼블릭 스페이스`, `부대 및 복리시설`, `단면도`.
- The top band uses exterior reference images and explains that public spaces between residential buildings become characterful park/community areas.
- The first site diagram marks linked green public spaces along the contour-following residential layout. Legend: dotted line `연결`; green fill `퍼블릭 스페이스`.
- The second site diagram overlays community/support facilities on selected public-space areas. Legend: orange line `부대 및 복리시설`; green fill `퍼블릭 스페이스`.
- The section diagram labels the hillside sequence in English: `EXPRESS BELTWAY`, `NEIGHBORHOOD PARK`, `MULTIFAMILY HOUSING`, `TERRACE HOUSING`, `ROAD`, `HOUSING`, `ROAD`.

## PDF p.192

![PDF page 192](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-192.jpg)

- Visible heading/caption: `부대 및 복리시설 Community Facilities`.
- The introductory note describes community facilities arranged with landscape programs such as `Playground`, `Tree zone`, `Rest Area`, and `Pond`.
- Three subarea panels are shown:

| Panel | Visible title | Main visible content |
|---|---|---|
| B5-1 | `Hilltop Community` | Dynamic topography; block placement follows terrain; a park and hilltop community center sit at the hilltop; the community building is integrated with the wooded landscape. |
| B5-2 | `Water Scape` | The main feature is a water-scape; the long open space is divided into an ecological park and a water-scape park for residents to enjoy nature. |
| B5-3 | `Health Lane` | A long park is paired with a community center containing a fitness club; roof garden/landscape rail connects toward mountain paths. |

- Each panel includes a contour/block diagram plus a rendered view. The lower wide image is labeled `입면도`.

## PDF p.193

![PDF page 193](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-193.jpg)

- Visible heading: `Terrace House Unit`.
- The page presents Level 1 through Level 5 terrace-house plans, exploded diagrams, a planted terrace rendering, and a lower section labeled `TERRACE HOUSE`.
- Diagram callouts include `COMMON AREA`, `MAIN ENTRANCE`, `PRIVATE AREA`, `TRELLIS COVERED RECESSES`, `GREENSCAPE`, `PRIVATE LEVEL`, and `COMMON LEVEL`.

| Level 5 | Th-3c (242㎡): 6세대; Th-3d (272㎡): 3세대; Th-3e (218㎡): 5세대 |

- Block-plan labels include `PLAYGROUND`, `HILLTOP PARK`, `LANDSCAPE PARK`, `WATER GARDEN`, `PLAYGROUND PARK`, and `HEALTH LANE`.

## PDF p.195

![PDF page 195](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-195.jpg)

- Visible headings: `보행자 동선`, `차량 동선`, `식재`.
- The top-left circulation diagram highlights pedestrian paths and public spaces; the top-right diagram highlights vehicle routes.
- The large central plan combines contours, roads, open-space bands, and block/parking labels including `P1-1`, `P1-2`, and `P2-1`.
- The bottom material/planting palette has visible labels: `Plant`, `Vine`, `Concrete 1`, `Concrete 2`, `Oak`, `Limestone`, `Stone`, `Wood`, `Green Roof`.
- The lower section continues the terrace/multifamily hillside relationship with material callout lines to the palette.

## PDF p.196

![PDF page 196](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-196.jpg)

- Visible headings: `비상 도로`, `바람 통로`.
- The top-left diagram marks emergency-road structure; the top-right diagram marks wind corridors across the hillside block.
- The main plan continues the contour-heavy site layout with labels including `P3-1` and `P3-2`, green open-space bands, and magenta directional arrows.
- The bottom landscape palette is labeled, left to right: `숲`, `벚꽃`, `잔디`, `포석`, `수공간`, `단풍`, `잔디경계`, `대나무`, `숲`.
- Visible landscape notes mention reflected sky/bamboo in water, the end of a bamboo grove visible from road and terrace, `공원내 우수지`, and `숲에서 시작되는 수공간`.

## PDF p.197

![PDF page 197](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-197.jpg)

- Visible headings: `B5-1 Block`, `사업승인 배치도`.
- This is a detailed approval site plan for the B5-1 block. It shows contour lines, site boundary, neighboring park, district-unit planning boundary, roads, parking access, buildings, and open-space facilities.
- Readable labels include `근린공원`, `대지경계선`, `지구단위계획구역`, `지하주차장-2`, `어린이놀이터 (304M2)`, `101동`, `102동`, `103동`, `106동`, and `107동`.
- Several small road/level dimensions are printed, but most unit labels and plan annotations are too dense for reliable full transcription from the page image.

## PDF p.198

![PDF page 198](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-198.jpg)

- Visible heading: `국제설계경기 프로젝트`.
- The page continues the B5-1 approval plan and includes a north arrow, topographic contour background, boundaries, road center/boundary lines, pedestrian-only road, emergency road, parking, and service rooms.
- Readable labels include `근린공원`, `대지경계선`, `지하주차 경계선`, `기계전기실`, `부대복리시설`, `지하주차장 (220대)`, `마루 저류조 (40ton)`, `10M 보행자 전용도로`, `도로경계선`, `건축한계선`, `도로중심선`, `보행자출입구`, and `4M비상도로`.
- Building labels visible in the plan include `104동`, `105동`, and `108동`; many individual unit labels are present but visually small.

## PDF p.199

![PDF page 199](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-199.jpg)

- Visible title block: `PANGYO HOUSING Competition`, `B5-2 BLOCK`.
- Design team block: `RIKEN YAMAMOTO & FIELD SHOP`; `Riken Yamamoto / Nahoko Yoshii / Motoki Yamamoto`; Korean partner `종합건축사사무소 건원` with listed Korean team names.
- Concept diagrams and captions:

| Visible caption | Notes |
|---|---|
| 자연경관과 어우러진 자연 일부로서의 단지계획 | Low-rise clusters preserve surrounding mountain form and scenery; cluster form responds to each slope condition. |
| 급경사지 식재계획 | Trees are planted on steep slopes to prevent soil loss and improve soil. |
| 클러스터 네트워크 조성 | Pedestrian-road networks connect clusters; outdoor plazas occur at road/path crossings. |
| 완만한 경사지 및 평지 식재계획 | Native species and deciduous trees are used near buildings and streets to make seasonal changes visible. |

- The lower maps include a slope/aspect classification diagram and a smaller overall concept plan. The green explanatory text block frames the proposal around building clusters as landscape and an intermediate community realm between private and public space; it is credited to `함인선`.

## PDF p.200

![PDF page 200](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-200.jpg)

- Visible headings/captions: `LAND USE KEYMAP`, `국제설계경기 프로젝트`.
- The keymap highlights the B5-2 location within the larger land-use plan.
- The main rendering shows low-rise clustered housing set into a green slope, with public paths and translucent human silhouettes.
- The lower green text explains three slope/orientation zones: gentle slopes `7~14°`, steep slopes `14~27°`, and north/west-facing slopes. It also describes clusters that bridge `3m`, `6m`, and `9m` level differences, excludes north/west slopes from building, and proposes two cluster types.
- Lower section captions: `Slope 7°~14°=Type A` and `Slope 14°~27°=Type B`. Small English labels include `room`, `shiki`, `communal deck`, `private garden`, and `parking`.

## Key Figures And Tables

- PDF p.191: Public-space and community-facility diagrams with section through express beltway, park, multifamily housing, terrace housing, and roads.
- PDF p.192: Three community-facility concept panels: `B5-1 Hilltop Community`, `B5-2 Water Scape`, `B5-3 Health Lane`.
- PDF p.193: `Terrace House Unit` level plans and unit-count schedule.
- PDF p.194: `Multi Family House Unit` level plans, unit-count schedule, block plan, and `Hilltop Park` / `Water Garden` / `Health Lane` details.
- PDF pp.195-196: Pedestrian, vehicle, emergency-road, wind-corridor, planting/material, and landscape-water diagrams.
- PDF pp.197-198: `B5-1 Block` approval site plan with boundaries, parking, community facilities, playground, service areas, and road controls.
- PDF p.199: `B5-2 BLOCK` concept diagrams by Riken Yamamoto & Field Shop and Kunwon.
- PDF p.200: `B5-2` land-use keymap, project stats table, cluster-type slope sections, and rendered perspective.

## Validation Notes

- Visual review only; no OCR tools, Live Text, image-to-text automation, or embedded text extraction were used.
- Reviewed source images `pdf-page-191.jpg` through `pdf-page-200.jpg`.
- All ten pages have `## PDF p.XXX` source markers and direct image links.
- Dense approval drawings on PDF pp.197-198 contain many small unit labels, spot elevations, dimensions, and boundary annotations; only clearly legible project labels and key quantitative items are captured here.
- The long explanatory text block on PDF p.199 is summarized rather than fully transcribed; visible heading, team, diagram captions, and core concepts are preserved.

# 국제설계경기 프로젝트: B5 Blocks And B5-2 Block Plans

Coverage note: PDF pp.201-210 continue the `03 판교신도시 건축계획안` / `국제설계경기 프로젝트` material. The visible content focuses on B5-1, B5-2, and B5-3 cluster housing, communal decks, water-friendly open space, community facilities, unit-type planning, and B5-2 block approval layout drawings. Text and labels were read visually from the page images without OCR.

## PDF p.201

![PDF page 201](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-201.jpg)

Site/cluster plan for B5-1 and B5-2 with green hillside background, internal roads, communal deck labels, neighborhood/community facilities, play areas, and main entrances.

Visible block/stat tables:

| Block | 클러스터수 | 주동개수 | 연립주택 | 테라스하우스 | 주차대수 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| B5-1 | 12 | 98 | 90 | 8 | 236 |
| B5-2 | 11 | 100 | 67 | 33 | 240 |

Visible labels and captions:

- `B5-1`, `B5-2`
- `Main Entrance`
- `Neighborhood Facility`
- `Community Facility`
- `Neighboring village`
- Repeated orange labels for `Communal Deck Level`; individual level/floor microtext is dense and only partly legible.

Figures: two small perspective/rendering images at the bottom show clustered housing in a wooded setting and a deck/courtyard scene.

## PDF p.202

![PDF page 202](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-202.jpg)

Continuation of the B5 site/cluster plan, centered on B5-3. The upper plan shows highway/park context, internal pedestrian routes, community facilities, children's play areas, and several main entrance labels.

Visible block/stat table:

| Block | 클러스터수 | 주동개수 | 연립주택 | 테라스하우스 | 주차대수 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| B5-3 | 10 | 102 | 92 | 10 | 245 |

Section diagrams at the bottom compare sloped housing/unit arrangements.

- Visible English note: `The combination of 10 different unit types, various siting plan is possible,`
- Unit labels visible above the sections include `unit-type B`, `unit-type D`, `unit-type B'`, `unit-type A`, and `unit-type J`.
- Section captions: `slope 7°-14° = Type A`; `slope 14°-27° = Type B`.
- Section labels include `underground parking`, `communal deck rooftop gardening`, `private garden`, `annex`, `bridge`, `horizontal louver`, `Shiki`, and `master room`.

Footer: `03 판교신도시 건축계획안 200 | 201`.

## PDF p.203

![PDF page 203](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-203.jpg)

Top row shows communal deck and `shiki` room diagrams, including labeled use examples:

- `Home Bar`
- `Atelier`
- `Music Room`
- `Billiards`

Heading: `친수형 단지`

Readable planning notes:

- B5-2 block has two characteristic water-friendly open-space elements: `수로` and `비오톱 연못 네트워크`.
- `수로`: uses water flowing from the mountain to form a waterway.
- `Silty Sand` is noted as highly permeable; waterproof sheet or concrete is proposed at the waterway floor because of landslide concern.
- `연못네트워크`: biotope groups follow the waterway; waterproof sheet and pea gravel finish are noted for biotope floors.
- A visible quantitative note says `분당 5리터` of water flows into the biotopes, providing drainage/wetland conditions for plants and animals.

Large perspective caption: `친수공간과 보행녹도측에서 바라본 전경`.

Bottom section diagram: housing section with `shiki`, `bridge`, private gardens, communal deck/madang deck, roof garden, underground parking, cross-ventilation/privacy notes, and environmental devices. Some small technical labels are visually dense.

## PDF p.204

![PDF page 204](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-204.jpg)

Top heading: `친환경 시공계획`.

Top diagram captions:

- `도로 조성`
- `침사조 설치`

Heading: `마루`

Visible text explains `마루 Shiki` as an expanded entry zone that communicates with floors and rooms, serving as a multipurpose space adaptable as `사랑채`, `홈오피스`, `아틀리에`, and `취미실`. It is also described as a buffer between communal deck space and private rooms; glass walls connect the interior with the outdoor environment.

Large perspective labels:

- `outer wall louver`
- `bridge`
- `atelier`
- `piano`
- `horizontal louver`
- `communal deck`

Bottom elevation/section drawing:

- Level labels include `roof level`, `4F level`, `3F level`, `2F Deck level`, and `1F Cluster level`.
- Repeated bay dimensions visible along the bottom include `6600` and one `8800`.

Footer: `03 판교신도시 건축계획안 202 | 203`.

# B5-3 Block - 국제설계경기 프로젝트

Coverage note: PDF pages 211-220 document the `PANGYO HOUSING Competition` proposal for `B5-3 BLOCK`. The pages include project credits, competition statistics, land-use key map, master-plan diagrams, sustainability and slope-response concepts, community/parking plans, unit types A-D, landscape and water-system strategies, circulation hierarchy, and site sections. Review was visual only; small diagram labels and compact tables are summarized when not fully legible.

## PDF p.211

![PDF page 211](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-211.jpg)

### PANGYO HOUSING Competition - B5-3 BLOCK

- Design team shown: `MACK Architect(s)` with Mark Mack / Matt Bean / Kyle Richardson.
- Korean partner shown: `(주)종합건축사사무소 동우건축`; visible staff names include 이홍억, 이재우, 허진, 박차환, 제상우, 강중구, 장지욱, 이재훈, 김미영, 김승희.
- Concept diagrams and captions:
  - `실내에서 바라본 전경`
  - `경사에 따른 주거 유형`
  - `지속가능한 친환경적 건축`
  - `공공 녹지 체계`
  - `보행 환경`
  - `전망과 프라이버시를 고려한 빌딩 타입`
- Bottom rendering shows low-rise and mid-rise housing clusters set into wooded hillside terrain, with stepped roofs and green terraces.

## PDF p.212

![PDF page 212](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-212.jpg)

### 국제설계경기 프로젝트

| Item | Visible Value |
|---|---:|
| 세대수 | 총 세대수 102세대 |
| 대지면적 | 29,798.99㎡ |
| 건축면적 | 10,002.82㎡ |
| 연면적 | 30,114.89㎡ |
| 건폐율 | 33.57% |
| 용적률 | 64.85% (19,322.57㎡) |
| 도로율 | 8.10% (2,424.00㎡) |
| 녹지면적률 | 28.90% (8,611.90㎡) |
| 자연지반율 | 18.70% (5,572.40㎡) |

- Left graphic: `LAND USE KEYMAP`, with the B5-3 area highlighted in green in a larger district silhouette.
- Main Korean text begins `판교 공동주택 공모전은` and frames the proposal as a new direction for Korean housing architecture that preserves distinctive nature and creates pedestrian-oriented environments.
- The text emphasizes maintaining existing landform, expanding surrounding spaces, preserving household independence, securing cool valley views, retaining natural forms, and using architectural promenades from the visual start of the site toward the hilltop.
- Bottom perspective continues the hillside housing cluster view.

## PDF p.213

![PDF page 213](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-213.jpg)

### Master Plan

- Top drawing: `Master Plan`, scale notation visible as approximately `Scale 1:2500`.
- Plan shows B5-3 as a long hillside site with contour lines, existing wooded areas, curving pedestrian routes, a blue watercourse, road access, and color-coded unit/building types labeled `A`, `B1`, `B2`, `C1`, `C2`, and `D`.
- Korean section heading: `경사에 따른 유형`.
- Text explains that three residential types are planned to respond to varied slopes and reduce site alteration: A+B for gentle slopes, C for steep slopes, and D on higher level ground.
- Bottom aerial rendering shows clustered housing stepping with the terrain, with darker forested slopes behind.

## PDF p.214

![PDF page 214](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-214.jpg)

### 지속가능한 환경친화적 건축

- Top plan continues the master plan across the eastern portion of the site, with contour lines, north arrow, forest edge, road, clustered residential blocks, and yellow/orange/green building-type coloring.
- Korean text describes sustainable ecological housing:
  - Each dwelling has a private garden.
  - Green space is shared with neighbors.
  - Garden and cluster green space help reduce summer heat and provide psychological stability.
  - Four-story row housing on the north side blocks cold winter winds.
  - All units are planned to face south for even sunlight.
  - Building planning includes natural ventilation through natural air and stair systems, electric/gas heating systems, efficient roof sections, and broad grass roofs.
- Bottom rendering shows stepped low-rise housing and mid-rise blocks embedded in wooded terrain.

## PDF p.215

![PDF page 215](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-215.jpg)

### 커뮤니티시설 / 주차장 / 입면도

- Top strip labels three facility themes: `Cultural Facilities`, `Social Activities`, and `Exercise Facilities`.
- `커뮤니티시설` plan maps B5-1, B5-2, and B5-3 blocks with a legend for `COMMUNITY`, `PLAYGROUND`, and `WATER SPACE`.
- Visible plan labels include `Entrance Plaza-1`, `Entrance Plaza-2`, `Entrance Plaza-3`, `Neighborhood Park`, `Cultural Facilities`, `Social Activities`, `Exercise Facilities`, `Playground`, and service/management/cafe/store labels; smaller labels are visually dense.
- `주차장` plan has a legend for `GROUND LEVEL PARKING` and `BASEMENT PARKING`, with parking-count annotations in cars and a compact B5-1/B5-2/B5-3 table at lower right. The table is present but the exact values are too small for confident transcription.
- `입면도` / `Overall Elevation & Section` shows an overall elevation across the hillside site against a mountain backdrop.

## PDF p.216

![PDF page 216](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-216.jpg)

### Community Facility Programs

| Block / Theme | Visible Program Areas |
|---|---|
| B5-1 Cultural Facilities | Neighborhood Stores: 61㎡; Management Office: 60㎡; Senior Club House: 30㎡; Information Center: 60㎡; Resident Cafeteria: 50㎡ |
| B5-2 Social Activities | Neighborhood Stores: 60㎡; Management Office: 60㎡; Senior Club House: 30㎡; Audio-Visual DVD Room: 60㎡; Resident Cafeteria: 50㎡ |
| B5-3 Exercise Facilities | Neighborhood Stores: 60㎡; Management Office: 60㎡; Senior Club House: 30㎡; Fitness Center: 90㎡; Health Spa: 108㎡ |

- Three rendered views show community buildings/plazas at each themed cluster.
- Lower plan excerpts highlight orange facility footprints. Readable labels include `Management Office`, `Community Plaza`, `Senior Club House`, `Resident Cafeteria`, `Audio-Visual DVD Room`, `Neighborhood Store`, and `Fitness center`.
- Bottom strip continues the overall elevation/section across the B5 site.

## PDF p.217

![PDF page 217](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-217.jpg)

### 세대별 유형/A+B 타입

- Top diagrams: `Type A&B`, `Type A`, and `Type B`.
- Unit plan captions: `Type A`, `Type B-1`, and `Type B-2`.
- Readable plan labels include `DINING RM`, `KITCHEN`, `LIVING RM`, `MASTER BED RM`, `BED RM`, `GARDEN`, and `COURT LIGHT`.
- Korean text states that A and B units are placed on the ground and arranged closely to provide a pedestrian environment between households.
- The description notes two-story units with kitchen/living room on the ground floor and main/children bedrooms on the second floor; internal and external spaces are arranged so outdoor cooking and conversation are possible.
- Lower diagrams include `UNIT TYPE-A`, `UNIT TYPE-B`, `East Elevation`, and `Longitudinal Section`.
- Bottom renderings are labeled `Unit Type-A` and `Unit Type-B`, showing terraced units, grass roofs, and yellow/orange facade accents.

## PDF p.218

![PDF page 218](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-218.jpg)

### 세대별 유형/C+D 타입

- Top diagrams: `Type C` and `Type D`.
- Unit plan captions: `Type C-1`, `Type C-2`, and `Type D`.
- Readable plan labels include kitchen, dining room, living room, master bedroom, bedroom, family room, terrace/balcony-like spaces, and circulation cores; fine interior labels are visually dense.
- Korean text describes C units as comparable to A/B in organization, with kitchen/living room and bedrooms arranged across levels, adapted to steeper slopes and terrace-house forms with large windows toward views.
- The text describes D type as row housing suited to valley views, with vertically stacked/varied unit bases, elevator access, lobby terraces overlooking adjacent public green, parking connected by lobby elevators, and roof spaces used for shade and leisure.
- Lower drawings: `East Elevation`, `UNIT TYPE-C`, and `UNIT TYPE-D`.
- Bottom renderings are labeled `Unit Type-C` and `Unit Type-D`.

## PDF p.219

![PDF page 219](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-219.jpg)

### Natural Form / Natural Formation & Landscape

- Top plan: `Natural Form`, showing the forest edge, existing waterway/start/end markers, resting spaces, playgrounds, red community/facility markers, and the hillside residential layout.
- Small tables titled `Green Area Ratio` and `Playground & Resting Area` list B5-1, B5-2, and B5-3 values; exact figures are visually dense at the provided page scale and are not transcribed.
- Middle heading: `Natural Formation & Landscape`.
- English captions:
  - `Application of the surrounding natural forest system to the outdoor planting design`
  - `Utilization of the existing water system into the outdoor landscape design`
- Legends/labels include `Existing Forest`, `Public Green Space`, `Private Green Space`, `Seasonal Waterways`, `Waterside Space`, `Natural Stream`, and `Open Ditch`.
- Renderings label landscape elements such as `Existing Forest`, `Public Green Space`, `Cafe Senior`, `Private Green Space`, `Seasonal Waterway`, and `Natural Stream`.
- Bottom drawings: `Cross Section` and `Section 01`, with level markers `+90`, `+100`, `+110`, and `+120`.

## PDF p.220

![PDF page 220](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-220.jpg)

### Circulation / Spatial Hierarchy

- Top diagrams include `Circulation` and `Spatial Hierarchy`.
- `Circulation` legend distinguishes `Driveway`, `Sidewalk`, and `Promenade`; adjacent text reads:
  - `Formation of safe pedestrian network`
  - `Provision of the well-landscaped green promenades without vehicle access`
- The circulation rendering labels `Promenade`, `Community Plaza`, `Driveway`, and `Sidewalk`.
- `Spatial Hierarchy` diagram shows `Public`, `Semi-Public`, and `Private` zones.
- Text describes a comprehensive spatial hierarchy from the housing-unit front yard to the community plaza and gradual transition from public to private space.
- The hierarchy rendering labels `Resting Space`, `Private Green Space`, `Cluster of Housing / Private Space`, and `Public Green Space`.
- Bottom drawings: `Section 02`, `Section 03`, and a small key map locating `Section 01`, `Section 02`, and `Section 03`; section level markers include `+90`, `+100`, `+110`, and `+120`.

# 국제설계경기 프로젝트 종료 및 턴키 프로젝트 시작

Coverage note: PDF pp.221-226 close the 국제설계경기 프로젝트 material with B5-3 Block approval/site-plan pages, participant jury-report pages, and the design competition guideline excerpt. PDF p.227 is a branding interstitial. PDF pp.228-230 begin the 턴키 프로젝트 section with the contents divider and the A21-1BL / 1공구 project opening.

## PDF p.221

![PDF page 221](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-221.jpg)

### B5-3 Block

### 사업승인 배치도

Large approval site plan for B5-3 Block. The plan shows north arrow, terrain contours, a red site boundary, building footprints, road/entry geometry, open-space facilities, and adjacent blocks/parks.

Readable plan labels and figures:

- 주민커뮤니티시설: `74.85㎡` appears more than once near the northern edge.
- 관리사무실: `151.07㎡`.
- 어린이놀이터: `366.60㎡`.
- 근린공원 is labeled beyond the upper/right boundary.
- 생태테라스 설치구간 is marked near the upper boundary.
- Building labels include `301`, `302`, `308`, `309`, with types such as `207(A)-6-1F` and mixed `207(B)` notation.
- Circulation labels include pedestrian/vehicle entry, underground parking entry, pedestrian road, and road boundary/setback notes.
- Visible widths include `15M 도로`, `10M 보행자전용도로`, and `6M` public/road boundary notes.

Figure note: the plan is dense with civil/elevation annotations and block labels. Major planning elements are legible, but many small symbols and elevation points should be checked on the page image if exact CAD-level detail is needed.

## PDF p.222

![PDF page 222](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-222.jpg)

### 국제설계경기 프로젝트

### 단지 종합배치도

Scale: `A1:1/500`, `A3:1/1000`.

This page continues the B5-3 Block site plan and includes the legend and notes. Readable building and facility labels include:

- `304 143-16-4F`.
- `305 207(A)+207(B)-10-1F`.
- `306 207(A)-4-1F`.
- `307 207(A)-6-1F`.
- 주민커뮤니티시설: `74.85㎡`.

## PDF p.223

![PDF page 223](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-223.jpg)

### 참가작 심사 보고서

The page presents jury-review text for three international design competition participants with submission-board images below each column.

### 벤 반 버클

Caption:

- 벤 반 버클(Ben van Berkel), UN Studio, 네덜란드 + (주)희림 종합건축사사무소.

Visible assessment themes:

- The review calls the stepped/twisting `꽈배기` plan creative and interesting.
- It notes a clear and systematic housing-development logic, with south-facing main residential spaces and north-facing service rooms.
- Concerns include difficulty understanding the unusual type, potentially inefficient linked corridors, limited public-space definition, and landscape/pedestrian spaces not fully adapted to the site context.

### 크리스틴 할리

Caption:

- 크리스틴 할리(Christine Hawley), Christine Hawley Architects, 영국 + (주)토문엔지니어링 건축사사무소.

Visible assessment themes:

- The review praises a sweeping/swept arrangement along contour lines and strong entrance-space composition with stepped terraces.
- It notes good placement of public spaces on the southern road edge and strong use of a central street as community core.
- Concerns include repeated front/back conditions along the linear arrangement and high-rise/low-rise terrace-house placement relationships.

### 켄 양

Caption:

- 켄 양(Ken Yeang), T.R.H + (주)유선건축 종합건축사사무소. The right column is partly cut by the page edge in this image, so the visible text is incomplete.

Visible assessment themes:

- The review discusses environmental-site-resource emphasis, courtyard/house-layout approaches, and natural-material/roof-garden ideas.
- Some paragraph text is partially outside the visible page crop; use the page image as the authoritative source for this column.

## PDF p.224

![PDF page 224](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-224.jpg)

### 참가작 심사 보고서 Continued

The leftmost visible strip continues the Ken Yeang review from PDF p.223. Two additional participant columns are fully visible.

### 엠브이알디브이

Caption:

- 위니 마스, 야곱 반 리스, 나탈리 드 브리스(Winy Maas, Jacob van Rijs, Nathalie de Vries), MVRDV, 네덜란드 + (주)디에이그룹 엔지니어링 종합건축사사무소 / (주)정림건축 종합건축사사무소.

Visible assessment themes:

- The review identifies this proposal as actively applying contour-direction placement.
- It praises regular and site-suited arrangement logic and strong linear form-making.
- It notes south-view and sunlight advantages, integrated/adjacent parking, and innovative vehicle circulation.
- Concerns include excessive rigidity, monotony, and insufficient use of the northern forest/open-space advantages.

### 마이클 지스코비츠와 칼라 코발스키

Caption:

- 마이클 지스코비츠, 칼라 코발스키(Michael Szyszkowitz, Karla Kowalski), Szyszkowitz-Kowalski, 독일 + (주)간우 건축사사무소.

Visible assessment themes:

- The review calls the natural green space between stepped residential buildings impressive.
- It notes dramatic contrast between views up the slope and views down the valley, and compact use of the site.
- Concerns include excessive east-west placement dependence, insufficient consideration of south-facing preferences in Korean housing, and limited usable exterior space for many units.

Page footer: `03 판교신도시 건축계획안 222 | 223`.

## PDF p.225

![PDF page 225](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-225.jpg)

### 설계경기 지침서

### 1. 총칙

Readable definition topics include 연립주택, 테라스하우스, 부대시설, 복리시설, 바닥면적, 전용면적, 공용면적, and 용적률. The terrace-house definition references a two-or-more household common housing type formed on sloped land with dedicated terraces at the front of units; the minimum terrace width is visible as `7M 이상`.

### 1.2. 계획 지표

#### 1.2.1. 지구 개요

- 설계명: 성남판교 택지개발지구 B5-1, 2, 3블럭 공동주택 부대복리시설 설계경기.
- 위치: 대한민국 성남시 분당구 판교동 일원 판교택지개발지구내.
- 용도지역: 일반주거지역(택지개발지구).

#### 1.2.2. 공모 대상 3개 블럭 세부 계획지표

| 블록 | 대지면적(㎡) | 건설호수(호) | 평균 평형 규모 | 허용용적률 | 최고층수(이하) | 유형 |
|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---|---|
| B5-1 | 28,594 | 98 | 연립주택: 50평 / 테라스하우스: 75평 | 65% | 4층 | 분양 |
| B5-2 | 29,135 | 100 | Same as above | 65% | 4층 | 분양 |
| B5-3 | 29,798 | 102 | Same as above | 65% | 4층 | 분양 |

Additional visible guidance:

- Planned household count should follow the table.
- Block unit mix may be planned within average unit-size ranges.
- 연립주택: `135~195㎡`, minimum two unit sizes.
- 테라스하우스: `218~278㎡`, minimum two unit sizes.
- 용적률 is `64% 이상 허용용적률 이하`, securing the maximum allowed FAR.

#### 1.2.3. 사업성 부문

- Applicants plan within a total estimated construction cost of approximately `87,000백만원`.

### 2. 개발의 목표 및 계획의 방향

Development themes:

- 자연친화 도시.
- 보행자 친화 도시.
- 개성있는 도시.

Development goals:

- 양호한 조망환경과 수경관의 보존.
- 환경보전과 친환경적 도시개발의 필요성.
- 자연친화적 중·저밀도 개발의 수요.

### 2.2. 계획의 방향

| Direction label | Visible content |
|---|---|
| Green City | 자연보호와 자연조건의 환경친화적 활용; 생태환경의 조성과 Green Network 형성 |
| Safe & Lively Community | 개성적이고 활성화된 커뮤니티 형성; 안전하고 쾌적한 공간 조성 |
| Green City | 지형과 어울리는 개성 있는 경관 창출; 주거동의 다양화와 형태적 조화 |

### 3. 세부지침

Visible subsections include `3.1. 일반사항`, `3.2. 단지계획`, `3.2.1. 건축물 배치 계획`, and `3.2.2. 동선 계획`.

Key visible planning requirements include:

- Main building orientation generally planned from south to west around `-60°` and to east around `90°`, based on road direction.
- Public landscape zones are to be assigned for comfortable pedestrian environments.
- Vehicle entry/exit should use provided traffic-improvement lines and district-unit-plan guidance.
- Pedestrian-only roads should connect at block boundaries and link to nearby parks, pedestrian-only roads, schools, and transit facilities.

## PDF p.226

![PDF page 226](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-226.jpg)

### 설계경기 지침서 Continued

### 3.2.3. 옥외공간 및 친환경 계획

Table:

| 적용 항목 | 적용률 | 적용 방법 |
|---|---:|---|
| 자연지반 녹지율 | 대지면적의 10% 이상 | 지하에 인공구조물이 없는 지반비율 |
| 녹지면적률 | 대지면적의 20% 이상 | 순수 식재가 가능한 면적 |
| 어린이 놀이터 | 300㎡ 이상 | 최소폭은 9M 이상 |
| 휴게공간 | 50㎡ 이상 | - |

Additional visible ecological guidance:

- Existing waterway use should link to the B5-2 block ecological watercourse and allow water flow naturally.
- Pond/rest areas connected to the ecological watercourse should include at least `40㎡` of lotus planting around the pond.

### 3.3. 건축계획

#### 3.3.1. 주거동계획

- Building form and placement should support natural resident exchange and contact.
- Common corridor/stair layouts should avoid infringing household privacy and support sunlight/ventilation.
- Terrace houses suited to ground contact are counted as first-floor placement.
- Outdoor access/approach planning is required for independent units on the third floor or higher.

#### 3.3.2. 단위세대계획

- Standard unit layout: 연립주택 `4LDK`, 테라스하우스 `4~5 LDK`.
- Unit heating: floor-heating structure.
- Master-bedroom attached spaces include bathroom, dressing room, powder room, and front balcony.
- Minimum visible ceiling height: `2.4m`.
- Duplex-type unit composition is permitted.

#### 3.3.4. 부대복리시설계획

Parking:

- Parking count: at least `2.4대` per household per block.
- Underground parking ratio, including deck parking, at least `90%`.
- Separate accessory-facility parking: one space per `150㎡`.
- Underground parking should be planned for direct natural lighting/ventilation where possible.
- Ramp internal turning radius: at least `5.5m`.
- Ramp slope: `14%` or less for curved ramps, `17%` or less for straight ramps.

Community center table:

| 시설 | 최소면적(전용) | 필요시설 |
|---|---:|---|
| 관리사무소 | 60㎡ | 사무실, 방재실(3×5M), 통신실(10㎡) |
| 경로당 | 30㎡ | 오락실 |

Resident common-facility table:

| 블럭 | 시설군 | 시설 | 최소면적(전용) | 필요부속시설 | 비고 |
|---|---|---|---:|---|---|
| B5-1 | 주민문화시설 | 주민정보센터 | 60㎡ | 컴퓨터책상, 열람대 | - |
| B5-1 | 주민카페테리아 | - | 50㎡ | 탕비시설 | - |
| B5-2 | 주민문화시설 | 시청각실(DVD룸) | 60㎡ | 오디오 및 비디오 시스템 | - |
| B5-2 | 주민카페테리아 | - | 50㎡ | 탕비시설 | - |
| B5-3 | 생활체육시설 | 피트니스센터 | 90㎡ | - | - |
| B5-3 | 생활체육시설 | 스파 | 100㎡ | 사우나, 욕조 구비 | 남녀구분 |

#### 3.3.5. 기타시설 계획

Visible requirements include underground water tanks of at least `1.5톤` per household by block, sufficient mechanical/electrical inspection space, unattended security facilities for residential/underground parking, district heating, no separate stormwater-treatment facility inside the complex, and planned energy-saving measures.

## Validation Notes

- Reviewed only `pdf-page-221.jpg` through `pdf-page-230.jpg` visually; no OCR tools, image-to-text automation, or embedded-text extraction were used.
- Source markers and image links are present for every page in the assigned range.
- PDF pp.221-222 contain dense architectural/civil plan annotations; only clearly legible labels and figures were transcribed.
- PDF pp.223-224 are jury-report pages with small images and dense paragraph text; participant names, captions, and visible assessment themes were captured without guessing unreadable microtext.
- PDF p.227 is intentionally mostly blank except for the `PAN-GYO Master Plan & Design Book` branding image.
- The range captures the section transition from the end of `국제설계경기 프로젝트` into `턴키 프로젝트`, with A21-1BL beginning on PDF p.229 and project statistics on PDF p.230.

# 턴키 프로젝트: A21-1 Block / 판교휴먼시아 어울림 (1공구)

Coverage note: PDF pp.231-236 continue the A21-1 Block turnkey project with marketing/theme strategy, site and landscape diagrams, exterior perspectives, the sales overview, and support/community-facility plans. PDF pp.237-240 present apartment unit-plan pages for 101㎡, 115㎡, 150㎡, and 180㎡ types with concept names, expanded/basic/variable plans, and interior mood images.

- Readable concept text:
  - `Massclusivity (Masstexclusivity)` as printed.
  - `VVIP Marketing`.
  - `소수를 위해 맞춤생산 방식으로 제공되는 고급품 및 고급 서비스`.
- Top strip includes three small massing/urban-design sketches.
- Main image is a dark-colored site plan showing towers and slab blocks around a broad north-south open-space corridor, tree rows, pedestrian paths, a watercourse/blue landscape element, and road edges.
- Left-side perspective/model image captions:

  - `중앙광장`

  - `환경친화적 경관의 연출 (C-D구간)`: 근린공원과 단지를 자연스럽게 연결하여 친환경 보행동선 조성.
  - `생활가로변의 확보 (D-A구간)`: 필로티 및 데크 설계로 보행자 편의 향상.

- Readable landscape/wellbeing notes:

  - `운중천과의 연계`: 단지내 조경시설을 운중천과 연결해 그린네트워크 체계 형성.
  - `자연 프로그램 도입`: 꽃밭만들기, 텃밭가꾸기 등 자연 체험.
  - `웰빙 테마정원 조성`: 예술적인 테라피원, 피트니스광장, 레크리에이션마당 조성.

# PDF pp.241-250 - 턴키 프로젝트: A26,27-1 Block

Coverage: this batch is within `03 판교신도시 건축계획안`, `턴키 프로젝트`, and documents the A26,27-1 Block proposal by Daelim Industrial and Woori Construction, presented as `판교휴먼시아 e-편한세상 (2공구)`.

## PDF p.241

![PDF page 241](../docs/pangyo-newtown-master-plan-design-book/assets/pages/pdf-page-241.jpg)

- Project heading: `PANGYO HOUSING Competition`, `A26,27-1 BLOCK`.
- Developer/contractor line: `대림산업 주식회사+우림건설(주)`.
- Design office: `(주)희림 종합건축사사무소`.
- Listed names: `정영균 / 박규용 / 이문림 / 이동원 / 황규익 / 서원호 / 장성근 / 송승원 / 권정일 / 박성민 / 유영무 / 류종현 / 김승주 / 김주희`.
- Main concept heading: `자연이 함께하고 삶이 풍요로운 곳...Enjoy of LOHAS`.
- Text describes three planning directions:
  - Nature-friendly space linked to the Dong-Pangyo water-circulation system and nearby neighborhood park.
  - Human-friendly community space through community facilities, plazas, safe pedestrian space, and barrier-free planning.
  - Landscape-friendly layout and residential-building planning to secure view corridors and near/mid/far landscape experiences.
- A26 block narrative notes view corridors linking existing northern complexes, middle/high schools, and detached-housing areas; tower and angled building forms are used for openness.
- A27 block narrative notes that tower-type building placement is used to improve household views and create a more open outdoor spatial structure; community facilities are organized around family/community plazas.
- Bottom diagrams/captions:
  - `Blue Network` and `Green Network`; bullets reference Dong-Pangyo water-circulation and green-network/view-corridor formation toward the neighborhood park.
  - Pedestrian/community diagram; bullets reference community facilities and plazas along the complex pedestrian route, plus safe barrier-free pedestrian space.
  - Landscape/view diagram; bullets reference maximizing views and securing view corridors.

## Validation Notes

- Visual review only; no OCR or image-to-text automation was used.
- Page images reviewed: `pdf-page-241.jpg` through `pdf-page-250.jpg`.
- The batch is filed under the guide's `턴키 프로젝트` range, which starts at PDF p.228.
- Several small plan annotations, core/unit labels, contour marks, and fine explanatory notes in elevation/design-callout strips are too small to read reliably from the page images; these are summarized as dense diagrams rather than guessed.
- The p.242 profile table is the main readable quantitative source in this batch; later pages are primarily diagrams, renderings, and planning concept boards.
